description: You are a cognitive neuroscience researcher preparing to run the Attention Network Test (ANT; Fan et al., 2002, J Cogn Neurosci) on a new participant cohort. A research assistant wrote a PEBL implementation of the ANT that contains several bugs introduced during adaptation from an earlier version. The broken script is at ~/pebl/tasks/ant/ant_task.pbl and the task cannot be run correctly in its current state. Your job: (1) identify and fix ALL parameter bugs in the script so it conforms to the standard ANT protocol from Fan et al. (2002), and (2) run the fixed task with the test participant using the command 'run-pebl ~/pebl/tasks/ant/ant_task.pbl' to confirm it executes without errors (use subject ID 'test01' and any session number when prompted). The ANT protocol specifies: cue-to-target SOA of 400 ms, four cue conditions (no_cue, center_cue, double_cue, spatial_cue), three flanker conditions (congruent, incongruent, neutral), and 3 test blocks. After running, save a bug report listing each bug you found and corrected to ~/pebl/tasks/ant/bug_report.txt.

metadata.bug_2: gCueTypes missing 'double_cue' (has only 3 of 4 cue conditions)
metadata.bug_3: gFlankerTypes missing 'neutral' (has only 2 of 3 flanker conditions)
metadata.reference: Fan et al. (2002). Testing the efficiency and independence of attentional networks. J Cogn Neurosci, 14(3), 340-347.
